extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the uphill finish in Lausanne with a late sprint, outgunning MATTHEWS after POGACAR's UAE team controlled the final climb without needing to attack.
tactical_patterns: UAE led POGACAR out for time bonus at 1km despite no GC threat, McNulty peeling off perfectly timed on 12% gradient.
Matthews positioned second wheel behind leadout but van Aert's raw power overcame textbook positioning on 12% finale ramp.
Breakaway caught because peloton crashes distracted chasers, then bunch immediately regrouped showing COVID depleted teams still controlled pace.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: Crashed twice early but UAE still strong enough to lead him out for bonus seconds, showing team depth concerns minimal
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Third consecutive Tour with multiple stage wins, now winning punchy uphill sprints on 12% gradients not just flat power finishes
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Second runner-up finish this Tour despite perfect positioning, lacking final 50m acceleration on steep gradients
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: positive: Third place for time bonus at 10-12% finish suggests GC ambitions, unusual for first-year Tour rider
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: Lost 9+ minutes on moderate climb day, major GC collapse compared to other contenders who only lost 4 seconds
